what_it_does: End-to-end test file for the E2 runtime scheduler. It builds a small two-constant-into-join graph and exercises scheduler.run through scenarios for reactive fan-in, compile-in-run, incremental change propagation, pause/resume, forced retry, and branched execution, checking results against a file store.
observations: Defines a reusable fanin(a,b) graph with two constants feeding a join node.
Uses a global ok flag and a check(label, cond) helper that prints PASS/FAIL lines.
Reads node outputs from the file store via run://g2/nid or run://g2/nid@branch addresses.
Expects scheduler.run to return a dict with keys ran, skipped, stuck, and compiled.
Recreates the store between the pause and earlier scenarios.
Tests six sequential scenarios in a single main() function.
standouts: Written as TDD before the E2 scheduler exists; docstring explicitly says it should be RED until E2 is built.
Treats pause, retry, and branch as addressing/control operations on the runtime namespace.
Asserts that compile() is wired into the run path by expecting r['compiled'] == 3.
conventions: Module-level docstring explains scope and run command.
Inserts parent directory into sys.path for local imports.
Uses shutil.rmtree for store reset between scenarios.
Helper functions separate graph construction, output lookup, and assertion logging.
concerns: Relies on an undocumented scheduler.run return contract (ran/skipped/stuck/compiled).
Global mutable ok makes the test stateful and harder to parallelize.
No final cleanup of the temporary store.
Branch address construction in out() uses f-string formatting not validated elsewhere.
notes: A TDD-style end-to-end contract test that sketches how the E2 scheduler should behave for reactive, incremental, and branched graph execution.
questions: What exact signature and return schema does scheduler.run implement?
How does the store map run://g2/nid@branch addresses to CAS content?
What does the compiled count represent, and how is it produced by the scheduler?
What node-type interface do constant, uppercase, and join expose?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it tests compile-in-run, larger/branching graphs, and pause/retry/branch as addressing operations.
apparent_themes: Pause/resume and forced re-execution as control primitives
intention_signals: To specify the expected behavioral contract of the E2 scheduler—reactive fan-in, incremental re-evaluation, integrated compilation, and branch-aware addressing—before the implementation is written.
novelty: Frames pause, retry, and branch as operations on a run:// address namespace and expects the scheduler to report how many ExecNodes were compiled during the run.
contribution: Provides a concrete, scenario-based acceptance test that defines the scheduler's expected semantics for reactive fan-in, change propagation, pause/resume, forced retry, and branched execution, along with helper graph construction and store-output inspection utilities.
summary_for_embedding: End-to-end TDD test for the E2 runtime scheduler covering reactive fan-in, compile-in-run, incremental change propagation, pause/resume, forced retry, and branched execution via store addressing.
intention_for_embedding: Specify and verify the scheduler's reactive, incremental, and branching execution semantics before the E2 implementation exists.

symbols.does: Accumulates a boolean condition into a global pass/fail flag and prints a PASS/FAIL line.
symbols.description: The check helper takes a label and a condition, updates the module-level ok variable, and prints a formatted result line so the test output is human-readable.

symbols.does: Constructs a two-constant-into-join graph with configurable constant values.
symbols.description: Returns a Graph containing two constant nodes and a join node, wired so both constants feed the join; the constant values default to 'A' and 'B' and are used across the E2 scenarios.

symbols.does: Reads a node's latest stored output content for a given branch.
symbols.description: Resolves the run:// address for a node, optionally with a branch suffix, fetches the head CAS reference from the store, and returns the stored content or None if no content exists.

symbols.does: Runs all E2 scheduler scenarios and returns an exit code reflecting overall pass/fail.
symbols.description: Sets up the file store, executes six scenarios (fan-in, compile wiring, change propagation, pause/resume, retry, and branching), checks each outcome, and prints a final pass/fail message.
